categories.label: Packages & software install
categories.blurb: Install, update, and remove software via OS package managers, language toolchains, and Windows update systems.

categories.label: Files, archives, filesystems
categories.blurb: Manage files, directories, archives, mounts, block devices, LVM, and Git checkouts as state.

categories.label: Services & scheduling
categories.blurb: Start, stop, and reload daemons; manage cron, scheduled tasks, beacons, and event triggers.

categories.label: Networking & firewalls
categories.blurb: Hosts files, network interfaces, firewall rules, and NAPALM-managed network device config.

categories.label: Users, auth, certificates, ACLs
categories.blurb: Local accounts, SSH/PGP keys, X.509 PKI, TLS, SELinux, Windows DACL/LGPO, mac keychain.

categories.label: System config & tuning
categories.blurb: sysctl, environment variables, locale, timezone, kernel modules, log rotation, Windows tasks.

categories.label: Salt-on-Salt, orchestration, meta
categories.blurb: Cross-minion orchestration, calling other configuration tools (Ansible, idem), and stateconf glue.

modules.name: alias
examples:
username:
  alias.present:
    - target: user@example.com

thomas:
  alias.present:
    - target: thomas@example.com
modules.summary: Configuration of email aliases

modules.name: ansiblegate
examples:
some_set_of_tasks:
  ansible:
    - system.ping
    - packaging.os.zypper
      - name: emacs
      - state: installed
modules.summary: Execution of Ansible modules from within states

modules.name: apache
examples:
/etc/httpd/conf.d/website.com.conf:
  apache.configfile:
    - config:
      - VirtualHost:
          this: '*:80'
          ServerName:
            - website.com
          ServerAlias:
            - www.website.com
            - dev.website.com
          ErrorLog: logs/website.com-error_log
          CustomLog: logs/website.com-access_log combined
          DocumentRoot: /var/www/vhosts/website.com
          Directory:
            this: /var/www/vhosts/website.com
            Order: Deny,Allow
            Deny from: all
            Allow from:
              - 127.0.0.1
              - 192.168.100.0/24
            Options:
              - Indexes
              - FollowSymlinks
            AllowOverride: All

/etc/httpd/conf.d/website.com.conf:
  apache.configfile:
    - config:
      - VirtualHost:
          - this: '*:80'
          - ServerName:
            - website.com
          - DocumentRoot: /var/www/vhosts/website.com
          - Directory:
              this: /var/www/vhosts/website.com
              Order: Deny,Allow
              Deny from: all
              Allow from:
                - 127.0.0.1
                - 192.168.100.0/24
              Options:
                - Indexes
                - FollowSymlinks
              AllowOverride: All
          - Directory:
            - this: /var/www/vhosts/website.com/private
            - Order: Deny,Allow
            - Deny from: all
            - Allow from:
              - 127.0.0.1
              - 192.168.100.0/24
            - If:
                this: some condition
                do: something
            - Else:
                this:
                do: something else
            - Else:
                this: " "
                do: another thing
modules.summary: Apache state

modules.name: apache_conf
examples:
Enable security conf:

modules.summary: Manage Apache Confs

examples:
# Store the current date in a file
'date > /tmp/salt-run':
  cmd.run

'> /var/log/messages/':
  cmd.run:
    - unless: echo 'foo' > /tmp/.test && rm -f /tmp/.test

functions.signature: (name, text=None, makedirs=False, source=None, source_hash=None, template='jinja', sources=None, source_hashes=None, defaults=None, context=None, header=None)

functions.name: copy_
functions.signature: (name, source, force=False, makedirs=False, preserve=False, user=None, group=None, mode=None, dir_mode=None, subdir=False, **kwargs)

functions.name: serialize
functions.signature: (name, dataset=None, dataset_pillar=None, user=None, group=None, mode=None, backup='', makedirs=False, show_changes=True, create=True, merge_if_exists=False, encoding=None, encoding_errors='strict', serializer=None, serializer_opts=None, deserializer_opts=None, check_cmd=None, tmp_dir=None, tmp_ext='', **kwargs)

functions.name: mknod
functions.signature: (name, ntype, major=0, minor=0, user=None, group=None, mode='0600')

functions.name: shortcut
functions.signature: (name, target, arguments=None, working_dir=None, description=None, icon_location=None, force=False, backupname=None, makedirs=False, user=None, **kwargs)

functions.name: cached
functions.signature: (name, source_hash='', source_hash_name=None, skip_verify=False, saltenv='base', use_etag=False, source_hash_sig=None, signed_by_any=None, signed_by_all=None, keyring=None, gnupghome=None, sig_backend='gpg')

examples:
/etc/foo.conf:
  file.managed:
    - source:
      - 'salt://foo.conf?saltenv=dev'
    - user: foo
    - group: users
    - mode: '0644'
    - attrs: i

salt_master_conf:
  file.managed:
    - user: root
    - group: root
    - mode: '0644'
    - names:
      - /etc/salt/master.d/master.conf:
        - source: salt://saltmaster/master.conf
      - /etc/salt/minion.d/minion-99.conf:
        - source: salt://saltmaster/minion.conf
modules.summary: Manage files and directories on disk — content, ownership, permissions, templating, line/block replacement. The most-used state module by a wide margin.

modules.name: firewall
modules.summary: State to check firewall configurations

modules.name: firewalld

functions.name: present
functions.signature: (name, block_icmp=None, prune_block_icmp=False, default=None, masquerade=None, ports=None, prune_ports=False, port_fwd=None, prune_port_fwd=False, services=None, prune_services=False, interfaces=None, prune_interfaces=False, sources=None, prune_sources=False, rich_rules=None, prune_rich_rules=False)

examples:
public:
  firewalld.present:
    - name: public
    - block_icmp:
      - echo-reply
      - echo-request
    - default: False
    - masquerade: True
    - ports:
      - 22/tcp
      - 25/tcp

my_zone:
  firewalld.present:
    - name: public
    - masquerade: True
    - port_fwd:
      - 22:2222:tcp
      - 80:443:tcp:192.168.0.1
modules.summary: Manage firewalld zones, services, and rules on RHEL/CentOS/Fedora — the modern replacement for raw iptables on those distros.

modules.name: iptables
examples:
httpd:
  iptables.append:
    - table: filter
    - chain: INPUT
    - jump: ACCEPT
    - match: state
    - connstate: NEW
    - dport: 80
    - protocol: tcp
    - sport: 1025:65535
    - save: True

accept_esp_in:
  iptables.append:
    - table: filter
    - chain: INPUT
    - jump: ACCEPT
    - source: 10.20.0.0/24
    - destination: 10.10.0.0/24
    - in-interface: eth0
    - match: policy
    - dir: in
    - pol: ipsec
    - reqid: 1
    - proto: esp
accept_esp_forward_in:
  iptables.append:
    - use:
      - iptables: accept_esp_in
    - chain: FORWARD
modules.summary: Manage Linux iptables firewall rules — chains, rules, policies, persistence.

examples:
us:
  keyboard.system

us:
  keyboard.xorg
modules.summary: Management of keyboard layouts

add_kvm:

remove_beep:

add_sound:

      - snd

modules.name: loop

functions.name: until_no_eval
functions.signature: (name, expected, compare_operator='eq', timeout=60, period=1, init_wait=0, args=None, kwargs=None)

examples:
wait_for_service_to_be_healthy:
  loop.until:
    - name: boto_elb.get_instance_health
    - condition: m_ret[0]['state'] == 'InService'
    - period: 5
    - timeout: 20
    - m_args:
      - {{ elb }}
    - m_kwargs:
        keyid: {{ access_key }}
        key: {{ secret_key }}
        instances: "{{ instance }}"

Wait for service to be healthy:
  loop.until_no_eval:
    - name: boto_elb.get_instance_health
    - expected: '0:state:InService'
    - compare_operator: data.subdict_match
    - period: 5
    - timeout: 20
    - args:
      - {{ elb }}
    - kwargs:
        keyid: {{ access_key }}
        key: {{ secret_key }}
        instances: "{{ instance }}"
modules.summary: Loop state

functions.name: lv_present

modules.name: mount

functions.name: mounted
functions.signature: (name, device, fstype, mkmnt=False, opts='defaults', dump=0, pass_num=0, config='/etc/fstab', persist=True, mount=True, user=None, match_on='auto', device_name_regex=None, extra_mount_invisible_options=None, extra_mount_invisible_keys=None, extra_mount_ignore_fs_keys=None, extra_mount_translate_options=None, hidden_opts=None, bind_mount_copy_active_opts=True, **kwargs)

functions.name: fstab_present
functions.signature: (name, fs_file, fs_vfstype, fs_mntops='defaults', fs_freq=0, fs_passno=0, mount_by=None, config='/etc/fstab', mount=True, match_on='auto', not_change=False, fs_mount=True)

examples:
/mnt/sdb:
  mount.mounted:
    - device: /dev/sdb1
    - fstype: ext4
    - mkmnt: True

functions.signature: (name, runas, dest_dir, spec, sources, tgt, template=None, deps=None, env=None, results=None, force=False, saltenv='base', log_dir='/var/log/salt/pkgbuild')

functions.name: repo
functions.signature: (name, keyid=None, env=None, use_passphrase=False, gnupghome='/etc/salt/gpgkeys', runas='builder', timeout=15.0)

examples:
salt_2015.5.2:
  pkgbuild.built:
    - runas: thatch
    - results:
      - salt-2015.5.2-2.el7.centos.noarch.rpm
      - salt-api-2015.5.2-2.el7.centos.noarch.rpm
      - salt-cloud-2015.5.2-2.el7.centos.noarch.rpm
      - salt-master-2015.5.2-2.el7.centos.noarch.rpm
      - salt-minion-2015.5.2-2.el7.centos.noarch.rpm
      - salt-ssh-2015.5.2-2.el7.centos.noarch.rpm
      - salt-syndic-2015.5.2-2.el7.centos.noarch.rpm
    - dest_dir: /tmp/pkg
    - spec: salt://pkg/salt/spec/salt.spec
    - template: jinja
    - deps:
      - salt://pkg/salt/sources/required_dependency.rpm
    - tgt: epel-7-x86_64
    - sources:
      - salt://pkg/salt/sources/logrotate.salt
      - salt://pkg/salt/sources/README.fedora
      - salt://pkg/salt/sources/salt-2015.5.2.tar.gz
      - salt://pkg/salt/sources/salt-2015.5.2-tests.patch
      - salt://pkg/salt/sources/salt-api
      - salt://pkg/salt/sources/salt-api.service
      - salt://pkg/salt/sources/salt-master
      - salt://pkg/salt/sources/salt-master.service
      - salt://pkg/salt/sources/salt-minion
      - salt://pkg/salt/sources/salt-minion.service
      - salt://pkg/salt/sources/saltpkg.sls
      - salt://pkg/salt/sources/salt-syndic
      - salt://pkg/salt/sources/salt-syndic.service
      - salt://pkg/salt/sources/SaltTesting-2015.5.8.tar.gz
/tmp/pkg:
  pkgbuild.repo

modules.name: pkgrepo
examples:
base:
  pkgrepo.managed:
    - humanname: CentOS-$releasever - Base
    - mirrorlist: http://mirrorlist.centos.org/?release=$releasever&arch=$basearch&repo=os
    - comments:
        - 'http://mirror.centos.org/centos/$releasever/os/$basearch/'
    - gpgcheck: 1
    - gpgkey: file:///etc/pki/rpm-gpg/RPM-GPG-KEY-CentOS-6

base:
  pkgrepo.managed:
    - name: deb http://ppa.launchpad.net/wolfnet/logstash/ubuntu precise main
    - file: /etc/apt/sources.list.d/logstash.list
    - keyid: 28B04E4A
    - keyserver: keyserver.ubuntu.com
    - require_in:
      - pkg: logstash

functions.signature: (name, venv_bin=None, requirements=None, system_site_packages=False, distribute=False, use_wheel=False, clear=False, python=None, extra_search_dir=None, never_download=None, prompt=None, user=None, cwd=None, index_url=None, extra_index_url=None, pre_releases=False, no_deps=False, pip_download=None, pip_download_cache=None, pip_exists_action=None, pip_ignore_installed=False, proxy=None, use_vt=False, env_vars=None, no_use_wheel=False, pip_upgrade=False, pip_pkgs=None, pip_no_cache_dir=False, pip_cache_dir=None, process_dependency_links=False, no_binary=None, **kwargs)

modules.name: win_appx
modules.summary: Remove or block Windows Appx packages — the modern Microsoft Store / UWP app format.

modules.name: win_certutil
examples:
salt://certs/cert.cer:
  certutil.add_store:
    - store: TrustedPublisher
modules.summary: Installing of certificates to the Windows Certificate Manager

modules.name: win_dacl
examples:
addAcl:
  win_dacl.present:
    - name: HKEY_LOCAL_MACHINE\SOFTWARE\mykey
    - objectType: Registry
    - user: FakeUser
    - permission: FullControl
    - acetype: ALLOW
    - propagation: KEY&SUBKEYS

removeAcl:
  win_dacl.absent:
    - name: HKEY_LOCAL_MACHINE\SOFTWARE\mykey
    - objectType: Registry
    - user: FakeUser
    - permission: FulLControl
    - acetype: ALLOW
    - propagation: KEY&SUBKEYS
modules.summary: Windows Object Access Control Lists

functions.name: feature_installed

modules.name: win_lgpo

functions.name: set_
functions.signature: (name, setting=None, policy_class=None, computer_policy=None, user_policy=None, cumulative_rights_assignments=True, adml_language='en-US', refresh_cache=False)

examples:
Ensure Account Lockout Duration:
  lgpo.set:
    - name: Account lockout duration
    - setting: 90
    - policy_class: Machine

Account lockout duration:
  lgpo.set:
    - setting: 120
    - policy_class: Machine

modules.name: win_network

functions.name: managed
functions.signature: (name, dns_proto=None, dns_servers=None, ip_proto=None, ip_addrs=None, gateway=None, enabled=True, **kwargs)

examples:
Local Area Connection #2:
  network.managed:
    - dns_proto: dhcp
    - ip_proto: dhcp

Local Area Connection #2:
  network.managed:
    - dns_proto: static
    - dns_servers:
      - 8.8.8.8
      - 8.8.4.4
    - ip_proto: static
    - ip_addrs:
      - 10.2.3.4/24

examples:
# Set timeout to 30 minutes on battery power
monitor:
    powercfg.set_timeout:
        - value: 30
        - power: dc

modules.name: x509

functions.name: private_key_managed
functions.signature: (name, bits=2048, passphrase=None, cipher='aes_128_cbc', new=False, overwrite=False, verbose=True, **kwargs)

functions.name: crl_managed
functions.signature: (name, signing_private_key, signing_private_key_passphrase=None, signing_cert=None, revoked=None, days_valid=100, digest='', days_remaining=30, include_expired=False, **kwargs)

examples:
peer:
  .*:
    - x509.sign_remote_certificate

base:
  '*':
    - cert
  'ca':
    - ca
  'www':
    - www
modules.summary: Legacy X.509 PKI module — generate keys, CSRs, sign against a Salt-managed CA. Prefer x509_v2 for new work.

modules.name: x509_v2

functions.name: certificate_managed
functions.signature: (name, days_remaining=None, ca_server=None, signing_policy=None, encoding='pem', append_certs=None, digest='sha256', signing_private_key=None, signing_private_key_passphrase=None, signing_cert=None, public_key=None, private_key=None, private_key_passphrase=None, csr=None, subject=None, serial_number=None, not_before=None, not_after=None, days_valid=None, pkcs12_passphrase=None, pkcs12_encryption_compat=False, pkcs12_friendlyname=None, **kwargs)

functions.name: crl_managed
functions.signature: (name, signing_private_key, revoked, days_remaining=None, signing_cert=None, signing_private_key_passphrase=None, include_expired=False, days_valid=None, digest='sha256', encoding='pem', extensions=None, **kwargs)

functions.name: private_key_managed
functions.signature: (name, algo='rsa', keysize=None, passphrase=None, encoding='pem', new=False, overwrite=False, pkcs12_encryption_compat=False, **kwargs)

examples:
base:
  '*':
    - cert
  'ca':
    - ca
  'www':
    - www
# /srv/salt/ca.sls
modules.summary: Modern X.509 PKI module — generate keys, CSRs, sign against a Salt-managed CA. Use this over x509 for new work.
